en.extract:
The wandering whistling duck (Dendrocygna arcuata) is a species of whistling duck. They inhabit tropical and subtropical Australia, the Philippines, Borneo, Indonesia, Papua New Guinea, Timor-Leste, and the Pacific Islands.


Taxonomy
There are three subspecies associated with this bird, D. a. arcuata (Indonesian wandering whistling duck), D. a. australis (Australian wandering whistling duck), and D. a. pygmaea (New Britain wandering whistling duck).


Description
Formerly named tree ducks, the wandering whistling duck has its new name because of their loud whistling calls and the whistling noise their wings make during flight. They have long necks and legs and look like a cross between a goose and a duck. They have a strong head and neck with a darker crown and hindneck. The breast contains black spotting and the feathers are mostly dark brown. They range in size from 54–60 cm in height and weigh on average 750 grams. They mainly feed on grasses, waterlilies, water plants and occasionally insects and aquatic vertebrae.


Habitat
The wandering whistling duck lives in deep lagoons, flooded grasslands or dams. They enjoy the water and rarely leave the shore. They can swim and dive with ease.


Breeding
Breeding occurs during the tropical wet season usually between December and May. During this time six to fifteen eggs are laid in a nest not far from water and usually in high grass or a sheltered area.


In Aboriginal language and culture
The Bininj people of western Arnhem Land call this animal djilikuybi in the Bininj Kunwok language, and hunt it for food. In the Kundjeyhmi dialect, it is known as djirrbiyuk, and there is an Aboriginal outstation in Kakadu National Park which is named Djirrbiyuk after this bird. In Yolŋu languages it is known as guyiyi or walkuli.


Gallery


References


External links

Dendrocygna spp
East Indian wandering whistling duck
Wandering Whistling Duck

The '''wandering whistling duck''' ('''''Dendrocygna arcuata''''') is a species of [[whistling duck]]. They inhabit tropical and subtropical [[Australia]], [[Philippines|the Philippines]], [[Borneo]], [[Indonesia]], [[Papua New Guinea]], [[Timor-Leste]], and the [[List of islands in the Pacific Ocean|Pacific Islands]].<ref name=pizzey />

==Taxonomy==
There are three subspecies associated with this bird, ''D. a. arcuata'' (Indonesian wandering whistling duck), ''D. a. australis'' (Australian wandering whistling duck), and ''D. a. pygmaea'' (New Britain wandering whistling duck).

==Description==
Formerly named tree ducks, the wandering whistling duck has its new name because of their loud whistling calls and the whistling noise their wings make during flight. They have long necks and legs and look like a cross between a [[goose]] and a [[duck]]. They have a strong head and neck with a darker crown and hindneck. The breast contains black spotting and the feathers are mostly dark brown. They range in size from 54–60 cm in height and weigh on average 750 grams. They mainly feed on grasses, waterlilies, water plants and occasionally insects and aquatic vertebrae.

==Habitat==
The wandering whistling duck lives in deep lagoons, flooded grasslands or dams. They enjoy the water and rarely leave the shore. They can swim and dive with ease.

==Breeding==
Breeding occurs during the tropical wet season usually between December and May. During this time six to fifteen eggs are laid in a nest not far from water and usually in high grass or a sheltered area.

==In Aboriginal language and culture==
The [[Bininj]] people of western [[Arnhem Land]] call this animal '''djilikuybi''' in the [[Bininj Gun-Wok|Bininj Kunwok]] language, and hunt it for food.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Garde |first1=Murray |title=djilikuybi |url=https://www.njamed.com/#djilikuybi |website=Bininj Kunwok Online Dictionary |publisher=Bininj Kunwok Regional Language Centre |access-date=7 Nov 2021}}</ref> In the [[Kundjeyhmi dialect]], it is known as '''djirrbiyuk''', and there is an Aboriginal outstation in [[Kakadu National Park]] which is named Djirrbiyuk after this bird.<ref>{{cite web |title=Wandering whistling duck |url=https://parksaustralia.gov.au/kakadu/discover/nature/birds/wandering-whistling-ducks/ |website=Kakadu National Park |access-date=7 November 2021}}</ref> In [[Yolŋu languages]] it is known as '''guyiyi'''<ref>{{cite web |title=guyiyi |url=https://yolngudictionary.cdu.edu.au/word_details.php?id=4347 |website=Yolŋu Matha dictionary |publisher=Charles Darwin University |access-date=7 November 2021}}</ref> or '''walkuli'''.<ref>{{cite web |title=walkuli |url=https://yolngudictionary.cdu.edu.au/word_details.php?id=7264 |website=Yolŋu Matha dictionary |publisher=Charles Darwin University |access-date=7 November 2021}}</ref>

zh.extract:
斑胸树鸭（台湾作胸斑樹鴨）（学名：Dendrocygna arcuata），是雁形目鸭科的一种鸟类。其种加词“arcuata”意为“弓形的”、“弯曲的”。


特征
斑胸树鸭体重约796.18克，翼长约186毫米，嘴峰长约42毫米，喙宽度约15.4毫米，喙厚度约14.1毫米，跗蹠长约45.4毫米，尾长约49.8毫米。斑胸树鸭是部分迁徙的鸟类，其栖息在开放的湖泊、沼泽等湿地环境中。食性为草食性，主要食物来源是水生植物。


亚种
Dendrocygna arcuata arcuata：分布于菲律宾至印度尼西亚。
Dendrocygna arcuata australis：分布于南新几内亚和澳大利亚北部和东部。
Dendrocygna arcuata pygmaea：分布于新不列颠岛（俾斯麦群岛）。
